questions.question:
Consider two rectangular sheets, Sheet M and Sheet N of dimensions 6 cm x 4 cm each.

Folding operation 1: The sheet is folded into half by joining the short edges of the current shape.
Folding operation 2: The sheet is folded into half by joining the long edges of the current shape.

Folding operation 1 is carried out on Sheet M three times.
Folding operation 2 is carried out on Sheet N three times.

The ratio of perimeters of the final folded shape of Sheet N to the final folded shape of Sheet M is ___________.

A. 13:7
B. 3:2
C. 7:5
D. 5:13
questions.to_find: Ratio of perimeter of Sheet N's final shape to Sheet M's final shape after 3 respective folding operations

understand.plain: Both sheets start as 6 cm x 4 cm rectangles. Sheet M uses operation 1 (joining short edges) three times, which repeatedly halves the longer dimension. Sheet N uses operation 2 (joining long edges) three times, which repeatedly halves the shorter dimension. After three folds each, we compute the perimeter of each resulting shape and find their ratio.

keywords.explain: Join the two short edges of the current shape together, halving the length along the longer dimension.
keywords.example: A 6x4 sheet folded with operation 1 becomes 3x4 (the 6 cm side is halved).

keywords.explain: Join the two long edges of the current shape together, halving the length along the shorter dimension.
keywords.example: A 6x4 sheet folded with operation 2 becomes 6x2 (the 4 cm side is halved).

keywords.explain: The total boundary length of a rectangle, equal to 2(length + width).
keywords.example: A 1.5 cm x 2 cm rectangle has perimeter 2(1.5+2) = 7 cm.

understand.visual_alt: Diagram showing the progressive folding of Sheet M (width halving each fold) and Sheet N (height halving each fold) over three operations.

given.aim: Find the ratio of the perimeter of Sheet N's final shape to Sheet M's final shape

terms.meaning: Both sheets are 6 cm (long side) x 4 cm (short side)
terms.example: Starting shape for both M and N: 6 cm x 4 cm rectangle
terms.connects: Determines which side is 'short' or 'long' at each fold step

terms.meaning: Join short edges, halving the longer dimension each time
terms.connects: Each fold divides the current longer dimension by 2

terms.meaning: Join long edges, halving the shorter dimension each time
terms.connects: Each fold divides the current shorter dimension by 2

terms.meaning: P = 2(l + w) for a rectangle of length l and width w
terms.example: Sheet M final 1.5x2: P = 2(1.5+2) = 7; Sheet N final 6x0.5: P = 2(6+0.5) = 13
terms.connects: Gives the boundary measurement used to form the ratio

given.plan: Track dimensions of each sheet through all three folds, then compute perimeters and simplify the ratio N:M.

steps.title: Fold Sheet M three times with Operation 1
steps.formula_raw: Join short edges => halve the longer dimension
steps.apply: Start: 6x4. After fold 1 (short edge = 4 cm, join -> new long = 6/2 = 3): 3x4. After fold 2 (short edge = 3 cm, join -> new long = 4/2 = 2): 3x2. After fold 3 (short edge = 2 cm, join -> new long = 3/2 = 1.5): 1.5x2.
steps.note: Each fold joins the two shortest edges, which halves the longer dimension of the current shape.

steps.title: Fold Sheet N three times with Operation 2
steps.formula_raw: Join long edges => halve the shorter dimension
steps.apply: Start: 6x4. After fold 1 (long edge = 6 cm, join -> new short = 4/2 = 2): 6x2. After fold 2 (long edge = 6 cm, join -> new short = 2/2 = 1): 6x1. After fold 3 (long edge = 6 cm, join -> new short = 1/2 = 0.5): 6x0.5.
steps.note: Each fold joins the two longest edges, which halves the shorter dimension of the current shape.

steps.title: Compute perimeters of final shapes
steps.apply: Sheet M final shape 1.5 x 2: P_M = 2(1.5 + 2) = 2 x 3.5 = 7 cm. Sheet N final shape 6 x 0.5: P_N = 2(6 + 0.5) = 2 x 6.5 = 13 cm.
steps.note: Perimeter is simply the boundary of the resulting folded rectangle.

steps.title: Compute the ratio P_N : P_M
steps.apply: P_N : P_M = 13 : 7. This is already in lowest terms since gcd(13, 7) = 1.
steps.note: The answer is 13:7, which matches option A.

solution.result: The ratio of perimeters (Sheet N : Sheet M) = 13 : 7

questions.formula_note: The perimeter formula P = 2(l + w) is applied to the final dimensions of each folded rectangle to compare boundary lengths.

formulas.name: Perimeter of a Rectangle
formulas.plain: P = 2(l + w), where l is the length and w is the width of the rectangle
